devdoc.details: Invariant: totalAllocated == sum of unclaimed allocations,         and totalAllocated <= TITN.balanceOf(this) at all times.

addAllocations(address[],uint256[]).details: Reverts on zero address or zero amount entries to catch         caller-side mistakes early. Cancelled addresses can be         re-added (cancel deletes the mapping entry).

devdoc.title: TitnDistributor

addAllocations(address[],uint256[]).notice: Add allocations for a list of addresses. Addresses that         already have an allocation (claimed or unclaimed) are         silently skipped. Reverts if the contract does not hold         enough TITN to cover totalAllocated after the additions.

allocationOf(address).notice: View helper for an account's allocation.

cancelAllocation(address).notice: Cancel an unclaimed allocation and return its TITN to the         owner. The address is removed from the mapping so it can         be re-added later if desired.

claim().notice: Claim the caller's full allocation. One-shot per address.

totalAllocated().notice: Sum of currently-allocated, unclaimed TITN. Reserved for         recipients; only the surplus above this can be withdrawn         by the owner via withdrawExcess().

transferOwner(address).notice: Transfer ownership to a new address.

withdrawExcess(address,uint256).notice: Withdraw any TITN held by the contract above what is         reserved for unclaimed allocations. Useful for recovering         excess funds without disturbing the allocation accounting.

userdoc.notice: Owner-managed TITN allocation contract. Owner funds the contract         with TITN and assigns per-address allocations. Recipients pull         their allocation via claim(). Owner may cancel unclaimed         allocations to recover the funds.
